title: Evangelion Wallscrolls
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Wallscrolls             Quote Post by Redtophat » Thu Nov 22, 2012 12:24 pm So would anyone know where to get good, high quality ones? I'm asking because I can't seem to find any company made ones and the rest vary in quality on ebay so I don't wanna take my chances with a $30+ wallscroll that amy end up as total crap.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Nov 22, 2012 12:37 pm I have two wall scrolls featuring Asuka that I got at I-CON this past April for $15.00 each. They seem to be of decent quality to me. Most of the ones I've seen online cost around $30.00. Couldn't give you a company name, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 22, 2012 5:32 pm I have a mini poster of some of the pilots (Shinji, Asuka, Rei, Kaworu) if you'd be interested: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/562701/Premium-Sales-Items-ALL-must-go/#562701 Regarding wallscrolls, I think anime conventions would be your best bet.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Nov 25, 2012 12:53 am Anime wall scrolls in general are a dicey business because of the preponderance of bootlegs from China. The giveaway is usually the large size and the subject matter (e.g. use of non-standard or doujin images, although they will rip off official images also) and lack of company stickers, e.g. Gainax, on the scroll. I have both a fake and a legit example - the latter (Death & Rebirth) is smaller and printed on higher quality cloth with more vibrant colors than the fake, which is the Asuka and Rei. Another example of legit scrolls are much smaller versions that have been coming out more recently, such as this one , which is 36.4 x 51 cm. GE Animation has began putting out licensed versions of a lot of anime products, Eva included. They currently have 2 wall scrolls available - the character montage and Asuka in a phone booth. Both images are from back when the series first aired. However GE does not sell directly to the consumer so someone selling anime goods would need to carry their products. Rightstuf for example does have the Asuka scroll and I saw the character one at my local Kinokuniya Bookstore a couple months ago. I would expect cons to have a policy about not carrying bootlegs, but even then because wall scrolls are so problematic unless you know what to look for, I would not be surprised to see the dealers carrying fakes regardless. This is the primary reason why I got out of scrolls years ago and nowadays rarely bother to even look at them.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Nov 25, 2012 1:25 am These are the two wall scrolls I found at I-CON: YA-252  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Nov 25, 2012 1:44 am The first one definitely seems like Sadamoto artwork it's very nice.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Sun Nov 25, 2012 1:55 am Thanks dudes, is the Z149 on this site legit? http://www.toysnjoys.com/eva_wall.html, I'm assuming it is but ya never know.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Nov 25, 2012 11:54 am ^That hyperlink you posted doesn't work because you included a comma. Here's a working link. Those all look pretty legit, but I wouldn't really know how to tell the difference. They're all the same price I bought mine for.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Nov 25, 2012 5:49 pm The first 2 examples are by GE Animation. Everything else is suspect and in particular the ones of Asuka and Rei in provocative poses (e.g. the Rei in Z246, which is from a hentai doujin by Mogudan ) are for sure fakes. Just measured my bootleg Asuka and Rei example and the fabric measures 30.5" x 40" not including the plastic parts. Z149 is slightly larger than this but my take is that everything there except for the first 2 scrolls is fake.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Sun Nov 25, 2012 6:31 pm I guess i'm getting the renewl scroll then, thanks fellas.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Nov 25, 2012 10:13 pm June wrote: Thanks dudes, is the Z149 on this site legit? http://www.toysnjoys.com/eva_wall.html, I'm assuming it is but ya never know.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Nov 26, 2012 10:48 am Sorry to hear about that, I appreciate the warning as I've thought about ordering from them.
